Env vars `GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT` + `GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET` + sha256 key\n251\t///    → `<endpoint>/file/<bucket>/<sha256>`\n252\tfn resolve_url(entry: &FixtureEntry) -> Result<String, String> {\n253\t    if let Some(ref url) = entry.url {\n254\t        return Ok(url.clone());\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    let endpoint = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\").map_err(|_| {\n258\t        \"no download URL in manifest entry and GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT is not set. \\\n259\t         Set GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT (e.g. https://f000.backblazeb2.com) and \\\n260\t         GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET, or add a 'url' field to the manifest entry\"\n261\t            .to_string()\n262\t    })?;\n263\t    let bucket = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\").map_err(|_| {\n264\t        \"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT is set but GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET is not\".to_string()\n265\t    })?;\n266\t\n267\t    let endpoint = endpoint.trim_end_matches('/');\n268\t    Ok(format!(\"{}/file/{}/{}\", endpoint, bucket, entry.sha256))\n269\t}\n270\t\n271\t/// Download URL to a file, returning the number of bytes written.\n272\tfn download(url: &str, dest: &Path) -> Result<u64, String> {\n273\t    // Build request; attach auth if env vars are set\n274\t    let mut req = ureq::get(url);\n275\t\n276\t    if let (Ok(account_id), Ok(app_key)) = (\n277\t        std::env::var(\"B2_ACCOUNT_ID\"),\n278\t        std::env::var(\"B2_APPLICATION_KEY\"),\n279\t    ) {\n280\t        let creds = format!(\"{}:{}\", account_id, app_key);\n281\t        // Base64 encode credentials\n282\t        let b64 = base64_encode(creds.as_bytes());\n283\t        req = req.set(\"Authorization\", &format!(\"Basic {b64}\"));\n284\t    }\n285\t\n286\t    let resp = req.call().map_err(|e| format!(\"HTTP GET {url}: {e}\"))?;\n287\t    if resp.status() != 200 {\n288\t        return Err(format!(\n289\t            \"HTTP {} from {url}\",\n290\t            resp.status()\n291\t        ));\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    let mut reader = resp.into_reader();\n295\t    let mut file = std::fs::File::create(dest)\n296\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot create {}: {e}\", dest.display()))?;\n297\t\n298\t    let mut buf = [0u8; Returns `Ok(true)` if it matches, `Ok(false)` if not,\n315\t/// `Err` if the file cannot be read.\n316\tfn verify_sha256(path: &Path, expected: &str) -> Result<bool, String> {\n317\t    let bytes =\n318\t        std::fs::read(path).map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read {}: {e}\", path.display()))?;\n319\t    let digest = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(&bytes));\n320\t    Ok(digest == expected)\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t/// Minimal base64 encoder (RFC 4648, standard alphabet, with padding).\n324\t/// Used for B2 Basic-auth header — avoids pulling in a base64 crate.\n325\tfn base64_encode(input: &[u8]) -> String {\n326\t    const TABLE: &[u8] = b\"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/\";\n327\t    let mut out = String::with_capacity(input.len().div_ceil(3) * 4);\n328\t    for chunk in input.chunks(3) {\n329\t        let b0 = chunk[0] as u32;\n330\t        let b1 = chunk.get(1).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n331\t        let b2 = chunk.get(2).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n332\t        let n = (b0 << 16) | (b1 << 8) | b2;\n333\t        out.push(TABLE[((n >> 18) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n334\t        out.push(TABLE[((n >> 12) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n335\t        if chunk.len() > 1 {\n336\t            out.push(TABLE[((n >> 6) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n337\t        } else {\n338\t            out.push('=');\n339\t        }\n340\t        if chunk.len() > 2 {\n341\t            out.push(TABLE[(n & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n342\t        } else {\n343\t            out.push('=');\n344\t        }\n345\t    }\n346\t    out\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// ── tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n350\t\n351\t#[cfg(test)]\n352\tmod tests {\n353\t    use super::*;\n354\t\n355\t    #[test]\n356\t    fn base64_encode_roundtrip() {\n357\t        // RFC 4648 test vectors\n358\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"\"), \"\");\n359\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"f\"), \"Zg==\");\n360\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"fo\"), \"Zm8=\");\n361\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foo\"), \"Zm9v\");\n362\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foob\"), \"Zm9vYg==\");\n363\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"fooba\"), \"Zm9vYmE=\");\n364\t        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foobar\"), \"Zm9vYmFy\");\n365\t    }\n366\t\n367\t    #[test]\n368\t    fn resolve_url_uses_explicit_url() {\n369\t        let entry = FixtureEntry {\n370\t            id: \"test\".into(),\n371\t            path: }\n380\t\n381\t    #[test]\n382\t    fn resolve_url_fails_without_env_or_url() {\n383\t        // Remove env vars if set\n384\t        std::env::remove_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\");\n385\t }\n414\t\n415\t    #[test]\n416\t    fn cmd_list_runs_without_panic() {\n417\t        // Just verify it doesn't crash; output goes to stdout\n418\t        // We can't assert output easily here, but we root = corpus_root();\n\n    println!(\n        \"{:<30}  {:<10}  {:<10}  path\",\n        \"id\", \"storage\", \"present\"\n    );\n    println!(\"{}\", \"-\".repeat(80));\n\n    for f in &fixtures {\n        let file_path = root.join(&f.path);\n        let present = if file_path.exists() {\n            \"yes\"\n        } else {\n            \"no\"\n        };\n        println!(\n            \"{:<30}  {:<10}  {:<10}  {}\",\n            f.id,\n            f.storage,\n            present,\n            file_path.display()\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!();\n    let total = fixtures.len();\n    let remote_count = fixtures.iter().filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\").count();\n    let missing_remote = fixtures\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\" && !root.join(&f.path).exists())\n        .count();\n    println!(\"{total} total fixtures, {remote_count} remote, {missing_remote} remote missing locally\");\n}\n\n// ── fetch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nfn cmd_fetch(args: &[String]) {\n    // Parse optional --fixture <id>\n    let mut specific_id: Option<String> = None;\n    let mut i = 0;\n    while i < args.len() {\n        match args[i].as_str() {\n            \"--fixture\" => {\n                i += 1;\n                specific_id = args.get(i).cloned();\n                if specific_id.is_none() {\n                    eprintln!(\"--fixture requires an argument\");\n                    std::process::exit(1);\n                }\n            }\n            other => {\n                eprintln!(\"unknown flag: {other:?}\");\n                eprintln!(\"usage: xtask corpus fetch [--fixture <id>]\");\n                std::process::exit(1);\n            }\n        }\n        i += 1;\n    }\n\n    let fixtures = match list_fixtures() {\n        Ok(f) => f,\n        Err(e) => {\n            eprintln!(\"corpus fetch: cannot load manifest: {e}\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Select fixtures to process\n    let targets: Vec<&FixtureEntry> = if let Some(ref id) = specific_id {\n        let matched: Vec<_> = fixtures.iter().filter(|f| &f.id == id).collect();\n        if matched.is_empty() {\n            eprintln!(\"corpus fetch: fixture '{id}' not found in manifest\");\n            std::process::exit(1);\n        }\n        matched\n    } else {\n        fixtures.iter().filter(|f| f.storage == \"remote\").collect()\n    };\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        println!(\"corpus fetch: no remote fixtures in manifest — nothing to do\");\n        return;\n    }\n\n    let root = corpus_root();\n    let mut fetched = 0usize;\n    let mut already_ok = 0usize;\n    let mut failed = 0usize;\n\n    for entry in &targets {\n        if entry.storage != \"remote\" {\n            // If --fixture was used on a non-remote entry, it's already inline\n            let path = root.join(&entry.path);\n            if path.exists() {\n                println!(\"[ok]      {} (storage={}, already present)\", entry.id, entry.storage);\n                already_ok += 1;\n            } else {\n                eprintln!(\"[missing] {} (storage={}, but file not found at {})\",\n                    entry.id, entry.storage, path.display());\n                failed += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let dest = root.join(&entry.path);\n\n        // Check if already present and valid\n        if dest.exists() {\n            match verify_sha256(&dest, &entry.sha256) {\n                Ok(true) => {\n                    println!(\"[ok]      {} (already present, sha256 matches)\", entry.id);\n                    already_ok += 1;\n                    continue;\n                }\n                Ok(false) => {\n                    eprintln!(\"[stale]   {} — sha256 mismatch, re-downloading\", entry.id);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    eprintln!(\"[warn]    {} — cannot read existing file: {e}; re-downloading\", entry.id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Determine download URL\n        let url = match resolve_url(entry) {\n            Ok(u) => u,\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — {e}\", entry.id);\n                failed += 1;\n                continue;\n            }\n        };\n\n        println!(\"[fetch]   {} from {}\", entry.id, url);\n\n        // Create parent directory if needed\n        if let Some(parent) = dest.parent() {\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(parent) {\n                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — cannot create directory {}: {e}\", entry.id, parent.display());\n                failed += 1;\n                continue;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Download\n        match download(&url, &dest) {\n            Ok(bytes_written) => {\n                // Verify sha256\n                match verify_sha256(&dest, &entry.sha256) {\n                    Ok(true) => {\n                        println!(\n                            \"[done]    {} — {:.1} KB written, sha256 ok\",\n                            entry.id,\n                            bytes_written as f64 / 1024.0\n                        );\n                        fetched += 1;\n                    }\n                    Ok(false) => {\n                        eprintln!(\n                            \"[error]   {} — downloaded {} bytes but sha256 mismatch (expected {})\",\n                            entry.id, bytes_written, entry.sha256\n                        );\n                        // Remove the bad file\n                        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&dest);\n                        failed += 1;\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — sha256 verify failed: {e}\", entry.id);\n                        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&dest);\n                        failed += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"[error]   {} — download failed: {e}\", entry.id);\n                failed += 1;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!();\n    println!(\"corpus fetch: {fetched} downloaded, {already_ok} already present, {failed} failed\");\n    if failed > 0 {\n        std::process::exit(1);\n    }\n}\n\n// ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Resolve the download URL for a remote fixture entry.\n///\n/// Priority:\n/// 1. `url` field in the manifest entry (explicit URL)\n/// 2. Env vars `GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT` + `GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET` + sha256 key\n///    → `<endpoint>/file/<bucket>/<sha256>`\nfn resolve_url(entry: &FixtureEntry) -> Result<String, String> {\n    if let Some(ref url) = entry.url {\n        return Ok(url.clone());\n    }\n\n    let endpoint = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\").map_err(|_| {\n        \"no download URL in manifest entry and GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT is not set. \\\n         Set GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT (e.g. https://f000.backblazeb2.com) and \\\n         GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET, or add a 'url' field to the manifest entry\"\n            .to_string()\n    })?;\n    let bucket = std::env::var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\").map_err(|_| {\n        \"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT is set but GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET is not\".to_string()\n    })?;\n\n    let endpoint = endpoint.trim_end_matches('/');\n    Ok(format!(\"{}/file/{}/{}\", endpoint, bucket, entry.sha256))\n}\n\n/// Download URL to a file, returning the number of bytes written.\nfn download(url: &str, dest: &Path) -> Result<u64, String> {\n    // Build request; attach auth if env vars are set\n    let mut req = ureq::get(url);\n\n    if let (Ok(account_id), Ok(app_key)) = (\n        std::env::var(\"B2_ACCOUNT_ID\"),\n        std::env::var(\"B2_APPLICATION_KEY\"),\n    ) {\n        let creds = format!(\"{}:{}\", account_id, app_key);\n        // Base64 encode credentials\n        let b64 = base64_encode(creds.as_bytes());\n        req = req.set(\"Authorization\", &format!(\"Basic {b64}\"));\n    }\n\n    let resp = req.call().map_err(|e| format!(\"HTTP GET {url}: {e}\"))?;\n    if resp.status() != 200 {\n        return Err(format!(\n            \"HTTP {} from {url}\",\n            resp.status()\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let mut reader = resp.into_reader();\n    let mut file = std::fs::File::create(dest)\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot create {}: {e}\", dest.display()))?;\n\n    let mut buf = [0u8; 65536];\n    let mut total = 0u64;\n    loop {\n        let n = std::io::Read::read(&mut reader, &mut buf)\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"read error: {e}\"))?;\n        if n == 0 {\n            break;\n        }\n        file.write_all(&buf[..n])\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"write error: {e}\"))?;\n        total += n as u64;\n    }\n\n    Ok(total)\n}\n\n/// Verify a file's SHA-256. Returns `Ok(true)` if it matches, `Ok(false)` if not,\n/// `Err` if the file cannot be read.\nfn verify_sha256(path: &Path, expected: &str) -> Result<bool, String> {\n    let bytes =\n        std::fs::read(path).map_err(|e| format!(\"cannot read {}: {e}\", path.display()))?;\n    let digest = format!(\"{:x}\", Sha256::digest(&bytes));\n    Ok(digest == expected)\n}\n\n/// Minimal base64 encoder (RFC 4648, standard alphabet, with padding).\n/// Used for B2 Basic-auth header — avoids pulling in a base64 crate.\nfn base64_encode(input: &[u8]) -> String {\n    const TABLE: &[u8] = b\"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/\";\n    let mut out = String::with_capacity(input.len().div_ceil(3) * 4);\n    for chunk in input.chunks(3) {\n        let b0 = chunk[0] as u32;\n        let b1 = chunk.get(1).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n        let b2 = chunk.get(2).copied().unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n        let n = (b0 << 16) | (b1 << 8) | b2;\n        out.push(TABLE[((n >> 18) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        out.push(TABLE[((n >> 12) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        if chunk.len() > 1 {\n            out.push(TABLE[((n >> 6) & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        } else {\n            out.push('=');\n        }\n        if chunk.len() > 2 {\n            out.push(TABLE[(n & 0x3f) as usize] as char);\n        } else {\n            out.push('=');\n        }\n    }\n    out\n}\n\n// ── tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn base64_encode_roundtrip() {\n        // RFC 4648 test vectors\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"\"), \"\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"f\"), \"Zg==\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"fo\"), \"Zm8=\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foo\"), \"Zm9v\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foob\"), \"Zm9vYg==\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"fooba\"), \"Zm9vYmE=\");\n        assert_eq!(base64_encode(b\"foobar\"), \"Zm9vYmFy\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn resolve_url_uses_explicit_url() {\n        let entry = FixtureEntry {\n            id: \"test\".into(),\n            path: \"large/test.grib2\".into(),\n            sha256: \"abc123\".into(),\n            size_bytes: 0,\n            storage: \"remote\".into(),\n            url: Some(\"https://example.com/test.grib2\".into()),\n        };\n        let url = resolve_url(&entry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(url, \"https://example.com/test.grib2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn resolve_url_fails_without_env_or_url() {\n        // Remove env vars if set\n        std::env::remove_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\");\n        std::env::remove_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\");\n        let entry = FixtureEntry {\n            id: \"test\".into(),\n            path: \"large/test.grib2\".into(),\n            sha256: \"abc123\".into(),\n            size_bytes: 0,\n            storage: \"remote\".into(),\n            url: None,\n        };\n        assert!(resolve_url(&entry).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn resolve_url_builds_b2_url_from_env() {\n        std::env::set_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\", \"https://f000.backblazeb2.com\");\n        std::env::set_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\", \"gribtract-corpus\");\n        let entry = FixtureEntry {\n            id: \"test\".into(),\n            path: \"large/test.grib2\".into(),\n            sha256: \"deadbeef\".into(),\n            size_bytes: 0,\n            storage: \"remote\".into(),\n            url: None,\n        };\n        let url = resolve_url(&entry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(url, \"https://f000.backblazeb2.com/file/gribtract-corpus/deadbeef\");\n        std::env::remove_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_ENDPOINT\");\n        std::env::remove_var(\"GRIBTRACT_B2_BUCKET\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn cmd_list_runs_without_panic() {\n        // Just verify it doesn't crash; output goes to stdout\n        // We can't assert output easily here, but we verify list_fixtures {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_43c8b6bf2a9548dabb08f10f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Parse-Speed Optimization Log\n2\t\n3\tRunning log of techniques tried for the fixed-station point-extraction benchmark.\n4\tFormat: technique → result → why (to avoid re-walking dead ends).\n5\t\n6\t## Baseline\n7\t\n8\t**Technique:** Full-grid decode (simple packing 5.0) → nearest-grid-point read.  \n9\t**Result:** Establishes baseline throughput. With the synthetic 5×5 inline fixture, US\n10\tmetro stations are outside the grid (0–40N, 0–40E), so `in_range = 0` and\n11\tthroughput is vacuously fast (no real grid lookups). Meaningful numbers require\n12\treal GFS data covering CONUS.  \n13\t**Why stopped:** This is the reference, not an optimization. Waiting for real data\n14\t(bead `bf-qfj`) before the throughput number is informative.\n15\t\n16\t---\n17\t\n18\t---\n19\t\n20\t## Attempt 1: Lazy/partial unpack for DRT=0 (simple packing)\n21\t\n22\t**Technique:** For template 5.0, point `i` lives at a fixed bit offset `i × bits_per_value`\n23\tin the Section 7 body — no inter-point dependencies.  Skip full-grid decode; instead store\n24\traw Section 7 bytes in `LazyField::section7_raw` and call `decode_point_drt0(body, packing, idx)`\n25\tper station.\n26\t\n27\t**Result:** Correctness confirmed on 5×5 fixture (all 25 points match full decode to tolerance).\n28\tCannot measure station-hours/s speedup: the only DRT=0 fixture in corpus (0-40N, 0-40E) has\n29\tno US metro stations in range.  The GFS 1-degree fixture is DRT=3, which does NOT support\n30\tlazy extraction (section7_raw is empty for DRT≠0).\n31\t\n32\t**Full-pipeline benefit (unmeasured):** Lazy path skips decoding the full grid (~65K float ops\n33\tfor a 1-degree field). Per-station bit extraction adds ~7 read_bits_at calls vs 7 vec[idx]\n34\taccesses.  Net win is positive only when we need a small number of points from a large field —\n35\tcannot confirm without a DRT=0 CONUS fixture.\n36\t\n37\t**Infrastructure kept:** `decode_point_drt0`, `LazyField`, `decode_bytes_lazy`, and\n38\t`run_lazy_nearest` bench are committed.  The bench runner calls `run_lazy_nearest` whenever\n39\tDRT=0 data is present, so the speedup will appear automatically when a CONUS DRT=0 fixture\n40\tis added to the corpus.\n41\t\n42\t**As expected:** Does NOT generalize to complex packing (DRT=2/3) — group-based coding means\n43\tpoint `i` is NOT at a fixed bit offset; `section7_raw` is intentionally left empty for DRT≠0.\n44\t\n45\t## Attempt 2: SIMD-like unpack via byte-aligned fast paths + loop refactor\n46\t\n47\t**Technique:** Two changes to `decode.rs`:\n48\t\n49\t1. **Byte-aligned fast paths in `unpack_n_bits`** — added `match n_bits { 8 | 16 | 32 => ... }`\n50\t   specializations that use direct byte/u16be/u32be reads instead of the general shift/mask\n51\t   loop. These expose the inner iterator loops to LLVM auto-vectorization.\n52\t\n53\t2. **DRT=3 group inner loop refactor** — two sub-changes:\n54\t   - Zero-width group fast path: when `w == 0`, fill with `gref` × `l` without any bit reads.\n55\t   - Precomputed bit offset: replaced the loop-carried `bit_offset` update with\n56\t     `start_bit + k * w` so LLVM can treat `k` as the induction variable and potentially\n57\t     vectorize the `read_bits_at` calls within a group.\n58\t\n59\t**Result:**\n60\t- DRT=0 (5×5 fixture, n_bits=8): 519K msg/s vs 422K baseline → **+23% throughput**, 106 MB/s vs 86 MB/s.\n61\t  The n_bits=8 fast path is the driver — direct byte copy, compiler auto-vectorizes.\n62\t- DRT=3 (GFS 1-degree, 65160 points): 313 msg/s vs 316 baseline → **no measurable change** (within noise).\n63\t  The precomputed-offset refactor did not move the DRT=3 needle: `read_bits_at` itself has\n64\t  too much internal branching (variable `bytes_needed`) for LLVM to vectorize it, even when\n65\t  the loop induction variable is dependency-free. The zero-width fast path may help on data\n66\t  with more w=0 groups, but not on this field.\n67\t- Agreement: 100% for DRT=0 and DRT=3 — no correctness regression.\n68\t\n69\t**Why stopped:** DRT=3 improvement requires a fundamentally different approach — either\n70\texplicitly unrolling the common group widths (e.g. dedicated extractors for w=12, w=8) or\n71\tusing `std::arch` SIMD intrinsics with `#[target_feature(enable=\"avx2\")]`. Not attempted here\n72\tto keep the change auditable and the correctness bar clear. The DRT=0 win is kept.\n73\t\n74\t**Implication for DRT=3 speed:** To materially speed up the DRT=3 inner loop, the next\n75\tattempt should specialize per-group extraction for the most common widths (typically w=0,\n76\t4, 8, 12 in GFS complex packing). A dispatch table keyed on `w` returning a closure that\n77\treads values directly (without the `bytes_needed` branch) would remove the last major\n78\tscalar bottleneck in `read_bits_at`.\n79\t\n80\t## Attempt 3: Parallelism across forecast-hour messages (rayon par_iter)\n81\t\n82\t**Technique:** Fan the per-forecast-hour `Field` objects across cores using `rayon::par_iter()`\n83\tzipped with the geometry cache rows. Each thread handles one field's N-station extractions,\n84\tresults summed. `Field` is `Send + Sync` (all fields are owned `Vec`s / primitive types).\n85\t\n86\t**Result:**\n87\t- nearest serial: 59,829,060 station-hours/s\n88\t- nearest-parallel (rayon): 1,344,086 station-hours/s → **44× SLOWER**\n89\t- bilinear serial: 16,018,307 station-hours/s\n90\t- bilinear-parallel (rayon): 1,272,033 station-hours/s → **12× SLOWER**\n91\t- Agreement: 100% nearest, 42.9% bilinear (same as serial — no correctness change)\n92\t\n93\t**Why it failed:** With only 2 fields and 7 stations per field, each rayon task is 7 Vec\n94\tindex operations (< 100 ns). Rayon's work-stealing queue overhead (microseconds per task)\n95\tcompletely swamps the payload. The parallel version is essentially measuring thread-scheduling\n96\tlatency, not extraction time.\n97\t\n98\t**When parallelism would help:** With 40+ forecast-hour messages (a full GFS f000-f120 cycle)\n99\tAND more expensive per-field work (e.g., DRT=3 decode-on-demand rather than pre-decoded vecs),\n100\tthe thread overhead becomes negligible. The geometry cache is already read-only and trivially\n101\tshared — the architecture is correct, just the current corpus is too thin to benefit.\n102\t\n103\t**Reverted:** No code kept. Rayon not added as dependency.\n104\t\n105\t**Prerequisite for a useful retry:** Real multi-message corpus (40+ messages from one GFS\n106\tcycle covering CONUS). Once that corpus exists, re-run with rayon on the pre-decoded\n107\t`&[Field]` slice — expected linear speedup to core count for the extraction loop.\n108\t\n109\t## Attempt 4: Left-aligned u64 sliding-window extractor for DRT=3 groups\n110\t\n111\t**Technique:** Replace the per-element `read_bits_at` calls inside each group's inner\n112\tloop in `decode_drt3` with a new `extract_group_windowed` function.  The function\n113\tmaintains a 64-bit buffer that is kept left-aligned (valid bits at the MSB end):\n114\t\n115\t- **Pre-fill:** load `ceil((skip + w) / 8)` bytes upfront so the buffer holds at\n116\t  least `(alignment_skip + w)` valid bits before the first extraction.\n117\t- **Extraction per element:** one `buf >> (64 - w) & mask` + `buf <<= w` — no\n118\t  division, no inner byte loop, no per-element `div_ceil`.\n119\t- **Refill:** `while buf_bits < w { load one byte }` runs at most `ceil(w/8) ≤ 4`\n120\t  times per element for `w ≤ 32`, amortised ~one byte load per 8 bits consumed.\n121\t\n122\t**Result:**\n123\t- DRT=3 (GFS 1-degree, ~65K points): **109.1 MB/s vs 92.8 MB/s baseline → +17.6%**\n124\t- grid_points/sec: 149M vs 127M → +17.3%\n125\t- Agreement: 100% — no correctness regression.\n126\t- New cross-validation test (`extract_group_windowed_matches_read_bits_at`) verifies\n127\t  all (skip=0..7, w=1..20) combinations against `read_bits_at` — all 140 cases pass.\n128\t\n129\t**Why it worked:** The original per-element path called `read_bits_at`, which\n130\tcomputed `bytes_needed = div_ceil(bit_start + n_bits, 8)` and then looped over\n131\t1–3 bytes to assemble `raw`.  Even with the loop unrolled, the branch over\n132\t`bytes_needed` (1 vs 2 vs 3 bytes) caused branch mispredictions.  The windowed\n133\tpath eliminates this branch entirely: for a given group, the refill pattern is\n134\tdetermined only by `w mod 8`, which the branch predictor can learn quickly (or LLVM\n135\tcan analyse at compile time).\n136\t\n137\t**Code kept:** `extract_group_windowed` in `crates/gribtract-core/src/decode.rs`.\n138\t`read_bits_at` is retained (used by `unpack_n_bits`, `decode_point_drt0`, and the\n139\tnon-group-value arrays in `decode_drt3`).\n140\t\n141\t**Remaining DRT=3 headroom:** the spatial differencing step (running sum over ~65K\n142\telements with a sequential dependency) is likely the next bottleneck — it cannot\n143\tbe vectorized and is O(n_points).  Possible next attempts:\n144\t- Prefix-sum SIMD approximation (non-trivial correctness risk).\n145\t- Avoid the intermediate `Vec<i64>` by combining bit extraction + spatial diff in\n146\t  one pass (saves one O(n) allocation and scan).\n147\t- Increase group-level parallelism if the GRIB2 encoder writes independent group\n148\t  blocks (format investigation needed).\n149\t\n150\t## Attempt 5: Merge spatial-diff pass and f64-scaling pass into one combined loop\n151\t\n152\t**Technique:** `decode_drt3` previously executed two separate O(n) passes after\n153\tbit extraction:\n154\t\n155\t1. In-place spatial-differencing over `packed: Vec<i64>` (reading and writing each element).\n156\t2. A separate scaling pass (`packed.iter().map(|&x| (r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d)`)\n157\t   that read each element a second time and wrote to `values: Vec<f64>`.\n158\t\n159\tFor a 65 K-point GFS field (`packed` ≈ 520 KB), `packed` exceeds a typical 256 KB L2\n160\tcache, so both passes read from L3/RAM.  The merge eliminates the second pass:\n161\t\n162\t- For `order == 0` (DRT=2): one `.iter()` loop — no change in complexity.\n163\t- For `order == 1`/`order == 2`: seed values (ival1, ival2) are emitted directly\n164\t  from the seed variables rather than overwriting `packed[0..order]`, then each\n165\t  subsequent `packed[i]` is read exactly once, the running-sum state is updated\n166\t  inline, and the final scaled `f64` is pushed to `values`.  `packed` is never\n167\t  mutated after construction.\n168\t\n169\t**Result (measured via `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml --release -- bench`):**\n170\t- DRT=3 (GFS fixture, 65 K-point field): **~128–130 MB/s vs 109.1 MB/s (Attempt-4 baseline) → +17–19%**\n171\t  (two consecutive runs: 127.1 MB/s terminal, 129.9 MB/s bench-results.json; run-to-run variation ±2%).\n172\t- Compared to pre-windowed baseline (92.8 MB/s): +38–40% cumulative (Attempts 4+5 combined).\n173\t- Agreement: 100% — no correctness regression (all tests pass; 100% agreement on DRT=3).\n174\t\n175\t**Why it worked:** The in-place spatial-diff pass wrote ~520 KB of i64 data back to\n176\tthe same Vec, then the scaling pass read it all again.  With two passes, the CPU\n177\tfetched `packed` twice from L3.  One combined pass fetches it once; the write-back\n178\tof the intermediate i64 values is eliminated entirely.\n179\t\n180\t**Code kept:** combined spatial-diff + scale loop in `decode_drt3`\n181\t(`crates/gribtract-core/src/decode.rs`).  The `packed` Vec remains (bit\n182\textraction requires the intermediate buffer), but its post-construction write path\n183\tis removed.\n184\t\n185\t**Next headroom:** The remaining bottleneck in `decode_drt3` is likely:\n186\t- The bit-extraction inner loop itself (`extract_group_windowed` refill loop).\n187\t- The spatial-diff running sum, which has a strict sequential dependency and\n188\t  cannot be SIMD-parallelized directly.\n189\t- Eliminating `packed` entirely (streaming from bit extraction directly to f64)\n190\t  would save the ~520 KB allocation and the single remaining read pass over it —\n191\t  feasible but requires restructuring the group loop to track diff state inline.\n192\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ea1addec-3b7b-43cb-b739-3c6611b73ae8","uuid":"97c9f588-9bb6-4f78-8aaf-b2f2361ec815","timestamp":"2026-07-02T15:32:48.011Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/gribtract/docs/notes/parse-speed-log.md","content":"# Parse-Speed Optimization Log\n\nRunning log of techniques tried for the fixed-station point-extraction benchmark.\nFormat: technique → result → why (to avoid re-walking dead ends).\n\n## Baseline\n\n**Technique:** Full-grid decode (simple packing 5.0) → nearest-grid-point read.  \n**Result:** Establishes baseline throughput. With the synthetic 5×5 inline fixture, US\nmetro stations are outside the grid (0–40N, 0–40E), so `in_range = 0` and\nthroughput is vacuously fast (no real grid lookups). Meaningful numbers require\nreal GFS data covering CONUS.  \n**Why stopped:** This is the reference, not an optimization. Waiting for real data\n(bead `bf-qfj`) before the throughput number is informative.\n\n---\n\n---\n\n## Attempt 1: Lazy/partial unpack for DRT=0 (simple packing)\n\n**Technique:** For template 5.0, point `i` lives at a fixed bit offset `i × bits_per_value`\nin the Section 7 body — no inter-point dependencies.  Skip full-grid decode; instead store\nraw Section 7 bytes in `LazyField::section7_raw` and call `decode_point_drt0(body, packing, idx)`\nper station.\n\n**Result:** Correctness confirmed on 5×5 fixture (all 25 points match full decode to tolerance).\nCannot measure station-hours/s speedup: the only DRT=0 fixture in corpus (0-40N, 0-40E) has\nno US metro stations in range.  The GFS 1-degree fixture is DRT=3, which does NOT support\nlazy extraction (section7_raw is empty for DRT≠0).\n\n**Full-pipeline benefit (unmeasured):** Lazy path skips decoding the full grid (~65K float ops\nfor a 1-degree field). Per-station bit extraction adds ~7 read_bits_at calls vs 7 vec[idx]\naccesses.  Net win is positive only when we need a small number of points from a large field —\ncannot confirm without a DRT=0 CONUS fixture.\n\n**Infrastructure kept:** `decode_point_drt0`, `LazyField`, `decode_bytes_lazy`, and\n`run_lazy_nearest` bench are committed.  The bench runner calls `run_lazy_nearest` whenever\nDRT=0 data is present, so the speedup will appear automatically when a CONUS DRT=0 fixture\nis added to the corpus.\n\n**As expected:** Does NOT generalize to complex packing (DRT=2/3) — group-based coding means\npoint `i` is NOT at a fixed bit offset; `section7_raw` is intentionally left empty for DRT≠0.\n\n## Attempt 2: SIMD-like unpack via byte-aligned fast paths + loop refactor\n\n**Technique:** Two changes to `decode.rs`:\n\n1. **Byte-aligned fast paths in `unpack_n_bits`** — added `match n_bits { 8 | 16 | 32 => ... }`\n   specializations that use direct byte/u16be/u32be reads instead of the general shift/mask\n   loop. These expose the inner iterator loops to LLVM auto-vectorization.\n\n2. **DRT=3 group inner loop refactor** — two sub-changes:\n   - Zero-width group fast path: when `w == 0`, fill with `gref` × `l` without any bit reads.\n   - Precomputed bit offset: replaced the loop-carried `bit_offset` update with\n     `start_bit + k * w` so LLVM can treat `k` as the induction variable and potentially\n     vectorize the `read_bits_at` calls within a group.\n\n**Result:**\n- DRT=0 (5×5 fixture, n_bits=8): 519K msg/s vs 422K baseline → **+23% throughput**, 106 MB/s vs 86 MB/s.\n  The n_bits=8 fast path is the driver — direct byte copy, compiler auto-vectorizes.\n- DRT=3 (GFS 1-degree, 65160 points): 313 msg/s vs 316 baseline → **no measurable change** (within noise).\n  The precomputed-offset refactor did not move the DRT=3 needle: `read_bits_at` itself has\n  too much internal branching (variable `bytes_needed`) for LLVM to vectorize it, even when\n  the loop induction variable is dependency-free. The zero-width fast path may help on data\n  with more w=0 groups, but not on this field.\n- Agreement: 100% for DRT=0 and DRT=3 — no correctness regression.\n\n**Why stopped:** DRT=3 improvement requires a fundamentally different approach — either\nexplicitly unrolling the common group widths (e.g. dedicated extractors for w=12, w=8) or\nusing `std::arch` SIMD intrinsics with `#[target_feature(enable=\"avx2\")]`. Not attempted here\nto keep the change auditable and the correctness bar clear. The DRT=0 win is kept.\n\n**Implication for DRT=3 speed:** To materially speed up the DRT=3 inner loop, the next\nattempt should specialize per-group extraction for the most common widths (typically w=0,\n4, 8, 12 in GFS complex packing). A dispatch table keyed on `w` returning a closure that\nreads values directly (without the `bytes_needed` branch) would remove the last major\nscalar bottleneck in `read_bits_at`.\n\n## Attempt 3: Parallelism across forecast-hour messages (rayon par_iter)\n\n**Technique:** Fan the per-forecast-hour `Field` objects across cores using `rayon::par_iter()`\nzipped with the geometry cache rows. Each thread handles one field's N-station extractions,\nresults summed. `Field` is `Send + Sync` (all fields are owned `Vec`s / primitive types).\n\n**Result:**\n- nearest serial: 59,829,060 station-hours/s\n- nearest-parallel (rayon): 1,344,086 station-hours/s → **44× SLOWER**\n- bilinear serial: 16,018,307 station-hours/s\n- bilinear-parallel (rayon): 1,272,033 station-hours/s → **12× SLOWER**\n- Agreement: 100% nearest, 42.9% bilinear (same as serial — no correctness change)\n\n**Why it failed:** With only 2 fields and 7 stations per field, each rayon task is 7 Vec\nindex operations (< 100 ns). Rayon's work-stealing queue overhead (microseconds per task)\ncompletely swamps the payload. The parallel version is essentially measuring thread-scheduling\nlatency, not extraction time.\n\n**When parallelism would help:** With 40+ forecast-hour messages (a full GFS f000-f120 cycle)\nAND more expensive per-field work (e.g., DRT=3 decode-on-demand rather than pre-decoded vecs),\nthe thread overhead becomes negligible. The geometry cache is already read-only and trivially\nshared — the architecture is correct, just the current corpus is too thin to benefit.\n\n**Reverted:** No code kept. Rayon not added as dependency.\n\n**Prerequisite for a useful retry:** Real multi-message corpus (40+ messages from one GFS\ncycle covering CONUS). Once that corpus exists, re-run with rayon on the pre-decoded\n`&[Field]` slice — expected linear speedup to core count for the extraction loop.\n\n## Attempt 4: Left-aligned u64 sliding-window extractor for DRT=3 groups\n\n**Technique:** Replace the per-element `read_bits_at` calls inside each group's inner\nloop in `decode_drt3` with a new `extract_group_windowed` function.  The function\nmaintains a 64-bit buffer that is kept left-aligned (valid bits at the MSB end):\n\n- **Pre-fill:** load `ceil((skip + w) / 8)` bytes upfront so the buffer holds at\n  least `(alignment_skip + w)` valid bits before the first extraction.\n- **Extraction per element:** one `buf >> (64 - w) & mask` + `buf <<= w` — no\n  division, no inner byte loop, no per-element `div_ceil`.\n- **Refill:** `while buf_bits < w { load one byte }` runs at most `ceil(w/8) ≤ 4`\n  times per element for `w ≤ 32`, amortised ~one byte load per 8 bits consumed.\n\n**Result:**\n- DRT=3 (GFS 1-degree, ~65K points): **109.1 MB/s vs 92.8 MB/s baseline → +17.6%**\n- grid_points/sec: 149M vs 127M → +17.3%\n- Agreement: 100% — no correctness regression.\n- New cross-validation test (`extract_group_windowed_matches_read_bits_at`) verifies\n  all (skip=0..7, w=1..20) combinations against `read_bits_at` — all 140 cases pass.\n\n**Why it worked:** The original per-element path called `read_bits_at`, which\ncomputed `bytes_needed = div_ceil(bit_start + n_bits, 8)` and then looped over\n1–3 bytes to assemble `raw`.  Even with the loop unrolled, the branch over\n`bytes_needed` (1 vs 2 vs 3 bytes) caused branch mispredictions.  The windowed\npath eliminates this branch entirely: for a given group, the refill pattern is\ndetermined only by `w mod 8`, which the branch predictor can learn quickly (or LLVM\ncan analyse at compile time).\n\n**Code kept:** `extract_group_windowed` in `crates/gribtract-core/src/decode.rs`.\n`read_bits_at` is retained (used by `unpack_n_bits`, `decode_point_drt0`, and the\nnon-group-value arrays in `decode_drt3`).\n\n**Remaining DRT=3 headroom:** the spatial differencing step (running sum over ~65K\nelements with a sequential dependency) is likely the next bottleneck — it cannot\nbe vectorized and is O(n_points).  Possible next attempts:\n- Prefix-sum SIMD approximation (non-trivial correctness risk).\n- Avoid the intermediate `Vec<i64>` by combining bit extraction + spatial diff in\n  one pass (saves one O(n) allocation and scan).\n- Increase group-level parallelism if the GRIB2 encoder writes independent group\n  blocks (format investigation needed).\n\n## Attempt 5: Merge spatial-diff pass and f64-scaling pass into one combined loop\n\n**Technique:** `decode_drt3` previously executed two separate O(n) passes after\nbit extraction:\n\n1. In-place spatial-differencing over `packed: Vec<i64>` (reading and writing each element).\n2. A separate scaling pass (`packed.iter().map(|&x| (r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d)`)\n   that read each element a second time and wrote to `values: Vec<f64>`.\n\nFor a 65 K-point GFS field (`packed` ≈ 520 KB), `packed` exceeds a typical 256 KB L2\ncache, so both passes read from L3/RAM.  The merge eliminates the second pass:\n\n- For `order == 0` (DRT=2): one `.iter()` loop — no change in complexity.\n- For `order == 1`/`order == 2`: seed values (ival1, ival2) are emitted directly\n  from the seed variables rather than overwriting `packed[0..order]`, then each\n  subsequent `packed[i]` is read exactly once, the running-sum state is updated\n  inline, and the final scaled `f64` is pushed to `values`.  `packed` is never\n  mutated after construction.\n\n**Result (measured via `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml --release -- bench`):**\n- DRT=3 (GFS fixture, 65 K-point field): **~128–130 MB/s vs 109.1 MB/s (Attempt-4 baseline) → +17–19%**\n  (two consecutive runs: 127.1 MB/s terminal, 129.9 MB/s bench-results.json; run-to-run variation ±2%).\n- Compared to pre-windowed baseline (92.8 MB/s): +38–40% cumulative (Attempts 4+5 combined).\n- Agreement: 100% — no correctness regression (all tests pass; 100% agreement on DRT=3).\n\n**Why it worked:** The in-place spatial-diff pass wrote ~520 KB of i64 data back to\nthe same Vec, then the scaling pass read it all again.  With two passes, the CPU\nfetched `packed` twice from L3.  One combined pass fetches it once; the write-back\nof the intermediate i64 values is eliminated entirely.\n\n**Code kept:** combined spatial-diff + scale loop in `decode_drt3`\n(`crates/gribtract-core/src/decode.rs`).  The `packed` Vec remains (bit\nextraction requires the intermediate buffer), but its post-construction write path\nis removed.\n\n**Next headroom:** The remaining bottleneck in `decode_drt3` is likely:\n- The bit-extraction inner loop itself (`extract_group_windowed` refill loop).\n- The spatial-diff running sum, which has a strict sequential dependency and\n  cannot be SIMD-parallelized directly.\n- Eliminating `packed` entirely (streaming from bit extraction directly to f64)\n  would save the ~520 KB allocation and the single remaining read pass over it —\n  feasible but requires restructuring the group loop to track diff state inline.\n","numLines":192,"startLine":1,"totalLines":192}}}

self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n78\t        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n79\t        println!(\n80\t            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} self.fixtures_matched);\n86\t        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n87\t        println!(\n88\t            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n89\t            self.fixtures_matched,\n90\t            comparable,\n91\t            self.agreement_pct()\n92\t        );\n93\t        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n94\t            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n95\t            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n96\t            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n97\t            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), );\n102\t            }\n103\t        }\n104\t        println!(\"=====================================\");\n105\t    }\n106\t}\n107\t\n108\t// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n109\t\n110\t/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\n111\tpub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n112\t    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n113\t\n114\t    macro_rules! check_meta {\n115\t        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n116\t            if $actual != $expected {\n117\t                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n118\t                    field: $field,\n119\t                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n120\t                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n121\t                });\n122\t            }\n123\t        };\n124\t    }\n125\t\n126\t    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n127\t    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n128\t    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n129\t    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n130\t    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n131\t\n132\t    // Forecast reference time\n133\t    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n134\t    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n135\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n136\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n137\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n138\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n139\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n140\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n141\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n142\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n143\t    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n144\t\n145\t    // Level\n146\t    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n147\t    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n148\t    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n149\t    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n150\t    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n151\t    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n152\t\n153\t    // Ensemble\n154\t    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n155\t        (None, None) => {}\n156\t        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n157\t            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n158\t            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n159\t        }\n160\t        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n161\t            field: \"ensemble\",\n162\t            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n163\t            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n164\t        }),\n165\t        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n166\t            field: \"ensemble\",\n167\t            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n168\t            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n169\t        }),\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    // Grid definition\n173\t    let ag = &actual.grid;\n174\t    let gg = &golden.grid;\n175\t    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n176\t    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n177\t    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n178\t    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n179\t    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n180\t    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n181\t    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n182\t    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n183\t    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n184\t    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(),   check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n188\t\n189\t    // Template identifiers\n190\t    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n191\t    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n192\t    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n193\t\n194\t    // Packing metadata\n195\t    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n196\t    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n197\t    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n198\t    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n199\t    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n200\t\n201\t    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n202\t        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    // Grid      actual: actual_pts.len(),\n214\t        };\n215\t    }\n216\t\n217\t    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n218\t    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n219\t        if a.1 != g.1 {\n220\t            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n221\t        }\n222\t    }\n223\t\n224\t    let     }\n229\t        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n230\t        if delta > tolerance {\n231\t            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n232\t                index: i,\n233\t                expected: g.0,\n234\t                actual: a.0,\n235\t                delta,\n236\t                tolerance,\n237\t            });\n238\t        }\n239\t    }\n240\t\n241\t    if    if result.is_match() {\n268\t            stat.matched += 1;\n269\t        } else {\n270\t            all_match = false;\n271\t        }\n272\t    }\n273\t Differential comparator: compares decoded `Field`s against golden references.\n//!\n//! Metadata fields are compared exactly; grid values are compared within the\n//! per-field tolerance derived from the packing header (half-ULP of the\n//! quantization step — see `docs/notes/oracle-and-tolerance.md`).\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse gribtract_core::types::Field;\nuse crate::golden::GoldenField;\n\n// ── Single-field comparison result ───────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A metadata field that did not match.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MetaMismatch {\n    pub field: &'static str,\n    pub expected: String,\n    pub actual: String,\n}\n\n/// A grid point that exceeded tolerance.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct PointMismatch {\n    pub index: usize,\n    pub expected: f64,\n    pub actual: f64,\n    pub delta: f64,\n    pub tolerance: f64,\n}\n\n/// Outcome of comparing one decoded `Field` to one `GoldenField`.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum FieldResult {\n    Match,\n    MetaMismatch(Vec<MetaMismatch>),\n    MaskMismatch { index: usize },\n    ValuesMismatch(Vec<PointMismatch>),\n    LengthMismatch { expected: usize, actual: usize },\n}\n\nimpl FieldResult {\n    pub fn is_match(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, FieldResult::Match)\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Coverage report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Per-(GDT,PDT,DRT) triple statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\npub struct TemplateStat {\n    pub attempts: u32,\n    pub matched: u32,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated outcome over all fixtures and fields in the corpus.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct CoverageReport {\n    pub fixtures_total: usize,\n    pub fixtures_matched: usize,\n    pub fixtures_decode_error: usize,\n    pub fixtures_no_golden: usize,\n    /// (gdt_template, pdt_template, drt_template) → stat\n    pub by_template: HashMap<(u16, u16, u16), TemplateStat>,\n}\n\nimpl CoverageReport {\n    pub fn agreement_pct(&self) -> f64 {\n        let denom = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        if denom == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        100.0 * self.fixtures_matched as f64 / denom as f64\n    }\n\n    pub fn print_report(&self) {\n        let comparable = self.fixtures_total.saturating_sub(self.fixtures_no_golden);\n        println!(\"=== Differential Harness Coverage ===\");\n        println!(\n            \"Fixtures : {} total  ({} comparable, {} no-golden)\",\n            self.fixtures_total,\n            comparable,\n            self.fixtures_no_golden\n        );\n        println!(\"  matched      : {}\", self.fixtures_matched);\n        println!(\"  decode errors: {}\", self.fixtures_decode_error);\n        println!(\n            \"Agreement: {}/{} ({:.1}%)\",\n            self.fixtures_matched,\n            comparable,\n            self.agreement_pct()\n        );\n        if !self.by_template.is_empty() {\n            println!(\"Per-template:\");\n            let mut templates: Vec<_> = self.by_template.iter().collect();\n            templates.sort_by_key(|(k, _)| *k);\n            for ((gdt, pdt, drt), stat) in templates {\n                println!(\n                    \"  GDT={gdt} PDT={pdt} DRT={drt}: {}/{}\",\n                    stat.matched, stat.attempts\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"=====================================\");\n    }\n}\n\n// ── Comparison logic ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Compare one decoded field against its golden counterpart.\npub fn compare_field(actual: &Field, golden: &GoldenField) -> FieldResult {\n    let mut meta_mismatches: Vec<MetaMismatch> = Vec::new();\n\n    macro_rules! check_meta {\n        ($field:literal, $actual:expr, $expected:expr) => {\n            if $actual != $expected {\n                meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n                    field: $field,\n                    expected: format!(\"{:?}\", $expected),\n                    actual: format!(\"{:?}\", $actual),\n                });\n            }\n        };\n    }\n\n    check_meta!(\"center\", actual.center, golden.center);\n    check_meta!(\"subcenter\", actual.subcenter, golden.subcenter);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.discipline\", actual.parameter.discipline, golden.parameter.discipline);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.category\", actual.parameter.category, golden.parameter.category);\n    check_meta!(\"parameter.number\", actual.parameter.number, golden.parameter.number);\n\n    // Forecast reference time\n    let rt = &actual.forecast.reference_time;\n    let grt = &golden.forecast.reference_time;\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.year\", rt.year, grt.year);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.month\", rt.month, grt.month);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.day\", rt.day, grt.day);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.hour\", rt.hour, grt.hour);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.minute\", rt.minute, grt.minute);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.second\", rt.second, grt.second);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.reference_time.significance\", rt.significance, grt.significance);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.time_range_unit\", actual.forecast.time_range_unit, golden.forecast.time_range_unit);\n    check_meta!(\"forecast.forecast_offset\", actual.forecast.forecast_offset, golden.forecast.forecast_offset);\n\n    // Level\n    check_meta!(\"level.type1\", actual.level.type1, golden.level.type1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor1\", actual.level.scale_factor1, golden.level.scale_factor1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value1\", actual.level.scaled_value1, golden.level.scaled_value1);\n    check_meta!(\"level.type2\", actual.level.type2, golden.level.type2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scale_factor2\", actual.level.scale_factor2, golden.level.scale_factor2);\n    check_meta!(\"level.scaled_value2\", actual.level.scaled_value2, golden.level.scaled_value2);\n\n    // Ensemble\n    match (&actual.ensemble, &golden.ensemble) {\n        (None, None) => {}\n        (Some(ae), Some(ge)) => {\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.member_type\", ae.member_type, ge.member_type);\n            check_meta!(\"ensemble.number\", ae.number, ge.number);\n        }\n        (None, Some(_)) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"None\".to_string(),\n        }),\n        (Some(_), None) => meta_mismatches.push(MetaMismatch {\n            field: \"ensemble\",\n            expected: \"None\".to_string(),\n            actual: \"Some(...)\".to_string(),\n        }),\n    }\n\n    // Grid definition\n    let ag = &actual.grid;\n    let gg = &golden.grid;\n    check_meta!(\"grid.template\", ag.template, gg.template);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.num_data_points\", ag.num_data_points, gg.num_data_points);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.nx\", ag.nx, gg.nx);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.ny\", ag.ny, gg.ny);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_first\", ag.lat_first.to_bits(), gg.lat_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_first\", ag.lon_first.to_bits(), gg.lon_first.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lat_last\", ag.lat_last.to_bits(), gg.lat_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.lon_last\", ag.lon_last.to_bits(), gg.lon_last.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.di\", ag.di.to_bits(), gg.di.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.dj\", ag.dj.to_bits(), gg.dj.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"grid.scanning_mode\", ag.scanning_mode, gg.scanning_mode);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.resolution_flags\", ag.resolution_flags, gg.resolution_flags);\n    check_meta!(\"grid.shape_of_earth\", ag.shape_of_earth, gg.shape_of_earth);\n\n    // Template identifiers\n    check_meta!(\"gdt_template\", actual.gdt_template, golden.gdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"pdt_template\", actual.pdt_template, golden.pdt_template);\n    check_meta!(\"drt_template\", actual.drt_template, golden.drt_template);\n\n    // Packing metadata\n    check_meta!(\"packing.reference_value\", actual.packing.reference_value.to_bits(), golden.packing.reference_value.to_bits());\n    check_meta!(\"packing.binary_scale_factor\", actual.packing.binary_scale_factor, golden.packing.binary_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.decimal_scale_factor\", actual.packing.decimal_scale_factor, golden.packing.decimal_scale_factor);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.bits_per_value\", actual.packing.bits_per_value, golden.packing.bits_per_value);\n    check_meta!(\"packing.original_field_type\", actual.packing.original_field_type, golden.packing.original_field_type);\n\n    if !meta_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        return FieldResult::MetaMismatch(meta_mismatches);\n    }\n\n    // Grid values\n    let tolerance = actual.packing.tolerance();\n    let actual_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = actual.values.iter().collect();\n    let golden_pts: Vec<(f64, bool)> = golden.values.iter().collect();\n\n    if actual_pts.len() != golden_pts.len() {\n        return FieldResult::LengthMismatch {\n            expected: golden_pts.len(),\n            actual: actual_pts.len(),\n        };\n    }\n\n    // Mask must match exactly; only present values are numerically compared.\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if a.1 != g.1 {\n            return FieldResult::MaskMismatch { index: i };\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut value_mismatches: Vec<PointMismatch> = Vec::new();\n    for (i, (a, g)) in actual_pts.iter().zip(golden_pts.iter()).enumerate() {\n        if !a.1 {\n            continue;\n        }\n        let delta = (a.0 - g.0).abs();\n        if delta > tolerance {\n            value_mismatches.push(PointMismatch {\n                index: i,\n                expected: g.0,\n                actual: a.0,\n                delta,\n                tolerance,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    if !value_mismatches.is_empty() {\n        FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(value_mismatches)\n    } else {\n        FieldResult::Match\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compare all decoded fields from one fixture against its golden reference.\n///\n/// Returns `true` if every field matches (count and content).\npub fn compare_fixture(\n    actual_fields: &[Field],\n    golden_fields: &[GoldenField],\n    report: &mut CoverageReport,\n) -> bool {\n    if actual_fields.len() != golden_fields.len() {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    let mut all_match = true;\n    for (actual, golden) in actual_fields.iter().zip(golden_fields.iter()) {\n        let key = (actual.gdt_template, actual.pdt_template, actual.drt_template);\n        let stat = report.by_template.entry(key).or_default();\n        stat.attempts += 1;\n\n        let result = compare_field(actual, golden);\n        if result.is_match() {\n            stat.matched += 1;\n        } else {\n            all_match = false;\n        }\n    }\n    all_match\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use gribtract_core::types::{\n        Field, ForecastTime, GridDefinition, GridProjection, GridValues, Level, PackingInfo,\n        ParameterId, ReferenceTime,\n    };\n    use crate::golden::{\n        GoldenField, GoldenForecastTime, GoldenGridDefinition, GoldenGridValues,\n        GoldenLevel, GoldenPackingInfo, GoldenParameterId, GoldenReferenceTime,\n    };\n\n    fn sample_field() -> Field {\n        Field {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: ParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: ForecastTime {\n                reference_time: ReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: Level {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6, projection: GridProjection::LatLon,\n            },\n            values: GridValues::Dense(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: PackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sample_golden(values: Vec<f64>) -> GoldenField {\n        GoldenField {\n            center: 7,\n            subcenter: 0,\n            parameter: GoldenParameterId { discipline: 0, category: 0, number: 0 },\n            forecast: GoldenForecastTime {\n                reference_time: GoldenReferenceTime {\n                    year: 2024, month: 6, day: 19,\n                    hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0,\n                    significance: 0,\n                },\n                time_range_unit: 1,\n                forecast_offset: 0,\n            },\n            level: GoldenLevel {\n                type1: 103, scale_factor1: 0, scaled_value1: 2,\n                type2: 255, scale_factor2: 0, scaled_value2: 0,\n            },\n            ensemble: None,\n            grid: GoldenGridDefinition {\n                template: 0, num_data_points: 4, nx: 2, ny: 2,\n                lat_first: 10.0, lon_first: 0.0, lat_last: 0.0, lon_last: 10.0,\n                di: 10.0, dj: 10.0, scanning_mode: 0, resolution_flags: 48,\n                shape_of_earth: 6,\n            },\n            values: GoldenGridValues::Dense(values),\n            gdt_template: 0,\n            pdt_template: 0,\n            drt_template: 0,\n            packing: GoldenPackingInfo {\n                reference_value: 270.0,\n                binary_scale_factor: 0,\n                decimal_scale_factor: 0,\n                bits_per_value: 8,\n                original_field_type: 0,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exact_match_returns_match() {\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn within_tolerance_returns_match() {\n        // tolerance = 0.5 * 2^0 / 10^0 = 0.5; delta of 0.3 should pass\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.3, 271.3, 272.3, 273.3]);\n        assert!(compare_field(&actual, &golden).is_match());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn exceeds_tolerance_returns_mismatch() {\n        // delta of 0.6 > tolerance 0.5\n        let actual = sample_field();\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 274.0]);\n        let result = compare_field(&actual, &golden);\n        // grid values match for indices 0-2; index 3: |273-274|=1 > 0.5\n        assert!(matches!(result, FieldResult::ValuesMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn meta_mismatch_detected() {\n        let mut actual = sample_field();\n        actual.center = 98; // wrong center\n        let golden = sample_golden(vec![270.0, 271.0, 272.0, 273.0]);\n        assert!(matches!(compare_field(&actual, &golden), FieldResult::MetaMismatch(_)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn coverage_report_agreement_pct() {\n        let report = CoverageReport {\n            fixtures_total: 4,\n            fixtures_matched: 2,\n            fixtures_no_golden: 1,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        // comparable = 4 - 1 = 3; matched = 2\n        assert!((report.agreement_pct() - 66.666).abs() < 0.01);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn zero_agreement_pct_when_no_comparable() {\n        let report = CoverageReport::default();\n        assert_eq!(report.agreement_pct(), 0.0);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":417,"startLine":1,"totalLines":417}}}

GEFS Ensemble PDT=4.1 (Priority 2)\n**Source**: NOAA GEFS v12 on AWS\n- Archive: https://noaa-gefs-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/index.html\n- File pattern: `gefs.YYYYMMDD/HH/atmos/gep01.tHHz.pgrb2.0p25.fFFF`\n- Expected: PDT 4.1 (individual ensemble member), global 0.25°\n- Target: Member 1 (control), forecast hour f000\n- Storage: remote\n\n### 3. Gaussian Grid GDT 3.40 (Priority 3)\n**Source**: NOAA GFS global Gaussian grid\n- Archive: https://noaa-gfs-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/index.html\n- File pattern: GFS on reduced Gaussian grid (if available) or extract specific field\n- Alternative: ECMWF IFS (uses Gaussian grids) if NOAA lacks GDT 3.40\n- Expected: GDT 3.40 (reduced Gaussian)\n- Storage: remote\n\n### 4. CONUS DRT=0 Simple Packing (Priority 4)\n**Source**: NOAA GFS CONUS subset or legacy file\n- Challenge: Modern GFS uses DRT=3; DRT=0 may require legacy archive\n- Alternative: Extract DRT=0 field from multi-message GFS file\n- Target: Any analysis field covering CONUS with simple packing\n- Storage: inline (expected to be small)\n\n### 5. Full GFS Cycle (Priority 5)\n**Source**: NOAA GFS 0.25 degree global\n- Archive: https://noaa-gfs-bdp-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/gfs.YYYYMMDD/HH/\n- Files: `gfs.tHHz.pgrb2.0p25.f000` through `gfs.tHHz.pgrb2.0p25.f120`\n- Expected: 40+ forecast hours for one field (e.g., TMP 2m)\n- Storage: remote (very large, ~2GB for full cycle)\n- Note: May be staged as follow-up due to size\n\n## Implementation Plan\n\n### Phase 1: Download and Verify\n1. Download sample files from each source to /tmp/\n2. Inspect with eccodes to verify GDT/PDT/DRT\n3. Extract single-field subsets if needed\n4. Compute SHA-256 and record metadata\n\n### Phase 2: Store and Generate Goldens\n1. Move verified files to tests/corpus/large/\n2. Run `python3 scripts/gen_golden.py <file> <fixture_id>`\n3. Store golden files in tests/corpus/golden/\n\n### Phase 3: Update Manifest\n1. Add manifest entries with:\n   - `id`: descriptive fixture ID\n   - `path`: large/<filename>.grib2\n   - `sha256`: computed hash\n   - `size_bytes`: file size\n   - `storage`: \"remote\"\n   - `provenance`: source URL and description\n2. For inline fixtures (small DRT=0), use storage=inline\n\n### Phase 4: Test Integration\n1. Run differential suite: `cargo test differential`\n2. Verify 100% agreement or create ratcheted fixtures\n3. Run station benchmark to verify CONUS coverage\n4. Update documentation as needed\n\n## Tools Required\n- Python3 with eccodes bindings ✓ (available)\n- curl/wget for downloads ✓ (available)\n- SHA-256 computation (scripts or standard tools)\n- Access to NOAA public archives ✓ (no auth needed)\n\n## Notes\n- eccodes is available for golden generation\n- Large fixtures will use storage=remote and fetched via `cargo xtask corpus fetch`\n- Need to ensure files are committed before upload to B2 (if using B2 storage)\n- Consider using direct URL in manifest for public NOAA sources\n\n## Sources\n- NAM: https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-nam/\n- GEFS: https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-gefs/\n- GFS: https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-gfs-bdp-pds/\n- NOAA NCEP products: https://www.nco.ncep.noaa.gov/pmb/products/\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}
